message:
test: add admin and public API route tests (bd-2sgg, bd-3s4n)

Admin API routes (9 test files, 99 tests):
- stats, users, incidents, reports, featured, tag-mappings
- appeals, appeals/[id], tickets, roadmap/[id], moderation/bulk-action

Public API routes (6 test files, 45 tests):
- vitals, ratings/summaries, reports, dmca, appeals

Total: 144 new tests across 15 files. All 2723 web tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task
Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature.

## Context
The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing.

## Test Scenarios
1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component**
   - Sidebar displays recent items
   - Clear history button works
   - Items link to correct pages
   - Empty state displays correctly
   - Loading state shows correctly

2. **History Page (/history)**
   - Page loads and displays items
   - Search/filter works
   - Clear all history works
   - Timestamps display correctly
   - Links navigate correctly

3. **History Tracking Integration**
   - Viewing a prompt adds to history
   - Search queries are tracked
   - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates
   - Items appear in sidebar after viewing

## Files to Modify
- e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests)
- e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors)

## Success Criteria
- All sidebar interactions tested
- History page fully tested
- Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete:
- Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests)
- History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation
- RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit
- History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display
- Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page
- 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vp1","title":"Bound share verify rate-limit bucket map size","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:20:38.438057764Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:49.892528794Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:29:49.892481957Z","close_reason":"Completed: migrated /api/share/[code]/verify from unbounded route-local map to shared bounded rate limiter with tests and full quality-gate validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background
Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention.

## Scope
- "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences.
- Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category".
- Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations.
- CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale.
- Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content).

## Deliverables
- Ranking pipeline and data model for signals.
- Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category).
- Digest email template + scheduling job.
- CLI command with JSON output.

## Test Requirements
### Unit Tests
- Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering.
- Preference filtering and exclusion rules.

### Integration Tests
- Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes.
- Digest job generates consistent results.

### E2E Tests
- Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview.
- CLI recommend -> matches feed top items.

## Success Criteria
- Users find relevant prompts faster.
- Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy.
- Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletWolf","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:07.759108222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:07.759072725Z","close_reason":"Completed OSS slice: core recommendation engine (signals + preferences) + web For You feed (/for-you) + /settings/recommendations prefs + CLI jfp recommend. Premium sync/persistence + any digest email job live in premium repo.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-2vsu","author":"VioletWolf","text":"Starting web-side unblocked slice of bd-2vsu: add explicit recommendation preferences (category boost + category hide) stored device-locally (localStorage key jfp_recommendation_preferences_v1). New Settings entry: /settings/recommendations, and For You section consumes preferences via getForYouRecommendations(). For You remains hidden when there is no activity AND no preferences (to avoid duplicating Featured), but will show once preferences are set even with zero history.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:50:18Z"}]}
